mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered instances or the t-statistic for return difference is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and real estate investment, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods show no statistically significant difference in forward returns compared to baseline periods at the 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and strong business investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive return frequency falls below 45% during industrial production uptrends or the information coefficient is negative over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Returns are not significantly negative when inflation exceeds the threshold, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates robust labor market absorption and wage growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict positive returns with a p-value below 0.05 in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects strong aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings momentum, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDP growth and 5-day forward returns is consistently below 0.01 across all rolling evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side weakness and reduced corporate output, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns during production contractions do not differ significantly from baseline periods at the 5% statistical significance level.
